JACKSON Records show conservative Republican U.S. Senate candidate Chris McDaniel voted Democrat 2003 state primary.
McDaniel others question accuracy records accuse Secretary State Delbert Hosemann, potential opponent, using state resources "opposition research" McDaniel.
Hosemann denies this said McDaniel trying deflect attention from voting record.
McDaniel said inspired teenager Ronald Reagan become lifelong Republican.
Republican Hosemann thought many waiting wings U.S. Senate seat, should incumbent Thad Cochran seek re-election.
Computerized voting records kept secretary state's office show McDaniel voted statewide Democratic primary 2003, appears have voted some Republican elections, including 2008 primary.
McDaniel, Republican state senator from Ellisville Jones County, says does recall voting such primary.
Tuesday, McDaniel's campaign spokesman Keith Plunkett said possible McDaniel voted 2003 Democratic primary only because, back then, most Jones County officials candidates were Democrats McDaniel would have wanted vote local offices.
Plunkett said McDaniel worked years help convince local Jones County officials switch parties, most Republican.
Jones County Circuit Clerk Bart Gavin said possible that clerk's office workers before taking office keyed wrong information into statewide computer system listing McDaniel voting Democratic 2003 primary.
records that would show sure, poll books from back then, have been destroyed.
Clerks required keep them only years.
Gavin said employee secretary state showed courthouse Jones County Tuesday wanting view poll books McDaniel's voting records.
said McDaniel campaign asked before look records, Monday holiday.
Gavin said hadn't arrived help man, before did, Hosemann called election commissioner.
Plunkett said appears Hosemann using employee political work taxpayer expense, which would violation state law.
Hosemann said employee, Nathan Upchurch, taking leave day, don't keep with what employees their time off."
said word some point that Upchurch being told clerk wasn't there could help him, called election commissioner knows could help.
"The real question here doesn't Chris (McDaniel) want people looking voting record?"
Hosemann said.
"When (The Clarion-Ledger) requested records, looked them, they show voted Democrat when Haley Barbour, Tuck, Tate Reeves Stacey Pickering were running Republican Primary.
They also show didn't vote presidential election 2004 when Bush running didn't vote Republican Primary 2008.
"His real issue voting record, whether someone looking records that open available public," Hosemann said.
